← all tutorials

Paxos Consensus — interactive PAXOS.md paxos.py

Three Proposers race to get a value chosen by a majority of Acceptors; a Learner is told the result. Step through Phase 1 (Prepare/Promise) and Phase 2 (Accept/Accepted), watch higher proposal numbers win, and see a late proposer get forced to reuse an already-chosen value — the whole safety argument. Every number is recomputed live in JS from the identical protocol as paxos.py.

① The message flow — step through a Paxos run

step 0 / 0
Prepare (1a) Promise (1b) Accept (2a) Accepted (2b) Rejected
How to read: arrows sweep left↔middle for Phase 1 & 2 between Proposer and Acceptors; Accepted arrows sweep right to the Learner. Each Acceptor's box shows its promised and accepted state updating live. Higher proposal number n always wins.

② Multi-Paxos — a stable leader halves the round-trips per value

What this shows: bare Paxos pays 2 round-trips per value (Phase 1 + Phase 2). A stable leader runs Phase 1 once (a lease), then only Phase 2 per value (1 round-trip). Drag K to watch the leader's advantage approach 2×.
Round-trips: basic = 2·K, multi = K+1 (one lease + one Phase 2 per value). Messages: basic = 4·N·K, multi = 2·N + 2·N·K. As K → ∞, multi/basic → ½.

③ Paxos vs Raft — same guarantee, different story

Both solve consensus for crash faults with a 2f+1 majority. Raft is Multi-Paxos with a built-in strong leader and a simpler explanation — Ongaro & Ousterhout's contribution is understandability, not power.
aspectPaxosRaft
leader modeldecentralized: any node can propose; no strong leader requiredstrong leader: ALL writes flow through one elected leader
rolesProposer / Acceptor / Learner (explicit, may be co-located)Leader / Follower / Candidate (one node's state machine)
core mechanismPrepare/Promise + Accept/Accepted (proposal numbers)RequestVote + AppendEntries (term numbers)
ordering termproposal number (round, id)term (monotonic integer)
understandabilitynotoriously subtledesigned to be teachable
fault modelcrash, 2f+1crash, 2f+1
log replicationMulti-Paxos: per-instance consensusbuilt-in: leader appends + matches
canonical paperLamport 1998/2001Ongaro & Ousterhout 2014
used byChubby, Spanner (Paxos), ZooKeeper (Zab)etcd, Consul, TiKV, CockroachDB
check: … JS Paxos recompute matches paxos.py GOLD (P2 forced to 'X', distinct chosen = 1, multi/basic RT).
